Global (All).title: Global Food Insecurity Spikes Amid Conflict and Drought
Global (All).summary: The World Food Programme reports a significant increase in global food insecurity, driven by conflict and drought in key regions, including Africa and the Middle East.

Global (All).title: Global Cyberattacks Rise as Russia-Ukraine Conflict Escalates
Global (All).summary: Cybersecurity agencies around the world report a surge in cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure, financial systems, and government institutions, linked to the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

Global (All).title: Global Economic Uncertainty Grows Amid Rising Inflation and Interest Rates
Global (All).summary: The International Monetary Fund warns of growing economic uncertainty, driven by rising inflation and interest rates, which may lead to a global recession if left unchecked.

United States.title: Biden Administration Faces Backlash Over Border Policy Changes
United States.summary: US President Joe Biden's administration is facing criticism from lawmakers and advocacy groups over its proposed changes to the country's border policies, sparking a new wave of tensions between the White House and Congress.

United States.title: US Lawmakers Propose Bill to Block TikTok Deal
United States.summary: US lawmakers have introduced a bill aimed at blocking the proposed deal between ByteDance and Oracle, which would allow China's TikTok to continue operating in the United States.

United States.title: New York City Schools to Close in Response to Teacher Shortage
United States.summary: The New York City Department of Education has announced that it will close several schools due to a severe teacher shortage, causing concerns about the impact on students' education and the city's education system as a whole.

South Korea.title: NKorea fires 2 ballistic missiles into Japan Sea
South Korea.summary: North Korea fired two ballistic missiles into the Japan Sea on June 26, with one of them flying within 100km of Japan. The move has raised concerns about a potential threat from Pyongyang.

South Korea.title: S. Korean President Yoon vows to strengthen military ties with US
South Korea.summary: South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol has reaffirmed his country's commitment to strengthening military ties with the US, with a focus on bolstering security cooperation in the face of growing North Korean threats.

South Korea.title: S. Koreans protest government's response to labor strikes
South Korea.summary: Thousands of South Koreans took to the streets to protest the government's handling of a recent wave of labor strikes, with many demanding greater job security and better working conditions.

Japan.title: Japan's Defense Ministry to Buy F-35B Fighters Worth 700 Billion Yen
Japan.summary: Japan's Defense Ministry plans to purchase 147 advanced F-35B fighters worth around 700 billion yen (around $6.7 billion) in an effort to strengthen its defense capabilities.

Japan.title: Protests Erupt in Japan Over Proposed Nuclear Reactor Restart
Japan.summary: Protesters in Japan have taken to the streets to express their opposition to the proposed restart of a nuclear reactor at Japan's Sendai nuclear power plant.

Japan.title: Ruling Party Loses Local Elections in Japan Amid Economic Uncertainty
Japan.summary: Japan's ruling party suffered losses in key local elections as voters voiced discontent with the government's handling of the economy and ongoing economic uncertainty.
